message:
style(ffs-cli): apply cargo fmt to cmd_evidence and cmd_repair

Purely cosmetic pass — no behavioural change, no logic touched. Running
cargo fmt on these two files reorders imports to alphabetical within
each `use` group, collapses some `assert!(err.to_string().contains(...))`
chains back onto a single `.` chain with the trailing paren style that
rustfmt prefers, and shuffles the re-export lists in `use super::{...}`
blocks into the same sort order rustfmt produces elsewhere in the
crate.

Keeps ffs-cli fmt-clean so `cargo fmt --check` in CI does not keep
flagging the same two files between unrelated feature commits.
